title: Intern - Key Clients and Institutions
description:
About Lighthouse Canton

Lighthouse Canton is an independent asset manager to institutional investors and private clients, with headquarters in Singapore. Our wealth management business serves ultra-high-net-worth individuals, families, and family offices across their investment, estate, tax, and philanthropic needs. The asset management business offers strong internal product capabilities in hedge funds, private equity, and traditional fundamental analysis-based strategies. Lighthouse Canton has offices in Singapore, Dubai, and Bengaluru India.
We believe in hiring smart and curious people with the highest integrity. We offer talented professionals an opportunity to grow in a collaborative environment, surrounded by driven colleagues, and the opportunity to make an impact from the first day in the organization.

Job Purpose

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented individual to join the Key Clients and Institutions team. Successful applicants will gain hands-on exposure to international banking and wealth management projects, providing an opportunity to work closely with seasoned professionals in a dynamic environment.

Job Responsibilities

  • Conduct research and extract relevant data to support key projects.
  • Organize, analyze and interpret data to support business objectives and project outcomes.
  • Stay informed on market trends and provide insights that will contribute to project development.
  • Assist in administrative tasks, including documentation, reporting, and data management.
  • Coordinate meetings, track project timelines, and collaborate with various stakeholders to support project execution.
  • Support the review and preparation of legal and compliance-related documentation under guidance.
  • Assist in maintaining proper records of contracts, agreements, and regulatory submissions.
  • Conduct basic legal research on regulatory requirements and industry standards to support the compliance and legal teams.
  • Ensure confidentiality and adherence to internal policies and regulatory frameworks when handling sensitive documents and data.

Qualifications & Skills Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, or a related field, with a strong academic record.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and knowledge of basic data analysis tools is a plus.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with an interest in financial markets and wealth management.
  • Detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of working independently.
  • Good time management and organizational skills.
  • Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with the ability to interact with different levels of stakeholders.

Internship Duration

  • Start immediately and commit for at least 3 months. 6 months is highly preferred.
